<findings>

[observation] [warning] 12 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.73) — 31 imports target "prd-domain-operations"
[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.27) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [warning] Cohesion of 0.27 is well below the 0.4 threshold — this zone aggregates unrelated concerns (MCP server, reshape logic, pruning, chunked review) that would benefit from separation.
[observation] [warning] Coupling of 0.73 is significantly above the 0.6 warning threshold, driven by 31 outbound imports to core and 26 to unit-analyze — this zone is highly dependent and fragile to upstream changes.
[observation] [warning] The cross-zone import path core → cli (13 imports) creates a cycle with cli → core (31 imports); core-layer code should never import from the CLI layer — these imports should be inverted or extracted to a shared module.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "cli-mcp-interface" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] reshape-reason.ts should be pinned to its correct zone (cli-mcp-interface or a dedicated reshape zone) via a sourcevision zone pin, rather than relying on directory co-location in src/analyze/ which makes its zone assignment invisible to filesystem navigation
[suggestion] [critical] src/cli/commands/constants.ts (9-zone hub) must be relocated to a foundation or schema zone before any cli-mcp-interface split is attempted — splitting cli-mcp-interface while constants.ts remains inside it will propagate the instability to all 9 consumers simultaneously
[suggestion] [critical] Zone "CLI and MCP Interface" (cli-mcp-interface) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.73, cohesion: 0.27, coupling: 0.73) — requires immediate architectural intervention
[pattern] [warning] The MCP server files (mcp.ts, mcp-tools.ts) and the reshape/prune batch-maintenance commands share no import edges with each other inside cli-mcp-interface — they are two independent sub-clusters held together only by the zone boundary. Splitting them into a dedicated mcp-server zone and a maintenance-commands zone would immediately raise cohesion in both resulting zones without changing any imports.
[relationship] [critical] cli-mcp-interface imports from core (31), unit-analyze (26), and unit-cli (13) simultaneously — it is the widest fan-out zone in the package. Unlike the orchestration-tier entry points (cli.js, ci.js) which are spawn-only, cli-mcp-interface imports directly from all downstream layers, making it both the highest-coupling zone and the one most resistant to incremental refactoring.
[anti-pattern] [warning] cli-mcp-interface bypasses the analyze pipeline's sequential abstraction by importing from both llm-analysis-engine (26) and prd-proposal-pipeline (5) rather than through a single facade — a narrow pipeline entry-point export would let the CLI drive the full pipeline without depending on its intermediate stages and would shrink cli-mcp-interface's coupling score by ~31 cross-zone imports

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.27) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.73) — 31 imports target "prd-domain-operations"
- 12 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- Cohesion of 0.27 is very low, reflecting that this zone mixes reshape-specific logic (reshape-reason.ts, reshape.ts), maintenance commands (prune.ts), interactive review (chunked-review.ts), and the MCP server — consider splitting MCP tooling (mcp.ts, mcp-tools.ts) into its own zone
- The bidirectional import cycle between cli and core (cli → core: 31 imports, core → cli: 13 imports) is a structural violation — core should not depend on CLI-layer code
- chunked-review-state.ts suggests stateful review sessions persisted between CLI invocations; verify this state file is cleaned up properly on completion or interruption to avoid stale state accumulation
- Cohesion of 0.27 is well below the 0.4 threshold — this zone aggregates unrelated concerns (MCP server, reshape logic, pruning, chunked review) that would benefit from separation.
- Coupling of 0.73 is significantly above the 0.6 warning threshold, driven by 31 outbound imports to core and 26 to unit-analyze — this zone is highly dependent and fragile to upstream changes.
- The cross-zone import path core → cli (13 imports) creates a cycle with cli → core (31 imports); core-layer code should never import from the CLI layer — these imports should be inverted or extracted to a shared module.
- Zone "cli-mcp-interface" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- cli-mcp-interface fans out to at least four zones simultaneously (core, unit-analyze, unit-cli, and the proposal pipeline) — it is functioning as an informal orchestration layer but without the spawn-only isolation that the architecture policy requires for orchestration-tier code. This pattern will silently grow as new CLI commands are added.
- The MCP server (mcp.ts, mcp-tools.ts) and the reshape/prune CLI commands in this zone have no shared data flow — they are architecturally unrelated files grouped together only because community detection co-located them. The MCP server's import needs pull in core and schema; reshape/prune pull in analyze utilities. These are two independent fan-out nodes masquerading as one zone.
- cli-mcp-interface imports from core (31), unit-analyze (26), and unit-cli (13) simultaneously — it is the widest fan-out zone in the package. Unlike the orchestration-tier entry points (cli.js, ci.js) which are spawn-only, cli-mcp-interface imports directly from all downstream layers, making it both the highest-coupling zone and the one most resistant to incremental refactoring.
- The MCP server files (mcp.ts, mcp-tools.ts) and the reshape/prune batch-maintenance commands share no import edges with each other inside cli-mcp-interface — they are two independent sub-clusters held together only by the zone boundary. Splitting them into a dedicated mcp-server zone and a maintenance-commands zone would immediately raise cohesion in both resulting zones without changing any imports.
- cli-mcp-interface imports directly from both llm-analysis-engine (26 imports) and prd-proposal-pipeline (5 imports), making it a participant in the analyze pipeline's internal implementation rather than a consumer of a single public entry point — if either intermediate zone changes its internal API surface, cli-mcp-interface breaks regardless of whether the high-level pipeline contract changed
- cli-mcp-interface bypasses the analyze pipeline's sequential abstraction by importing from both llm-analysis-engine (26) and prd-proposal-pipeline (5) rather than through a single facade — a narrow pipeline entry-point export would let the CLI drive the full pipeline without depending on its intermediate stages and would shrink cli-mcp-interface's coupling score by ~31 cross-zone imports
- src/cli/commands/constants.ts is simultaneously a 9-zone cross-cutting hub AND lives inside the lowest-cohesion zone in the package (0.27) — the hub's ownership is maximally unstable; any split or refactor of cli-mcp-interface that moves constants.ts requires coordinated updates across all 9 consumer zones at once
- reshape-reason.ts is physically located in src/analyze/ but zone-assigned to cli-mcp-interface — developers browsing src/analyze/ see a file that belongs to a different zone with no filesystem signal, and any new file added to src/analyze/ next to reshape-reason.ts will be arbitrarily absorbed into either cli-mcp-interface or the analyze zones depending on its first import
- src/cli/commands/constants.ts (9-zone hub) must be relocated to a foundation or schema zone before any cli-mcp-interface split is attempted — splitting cli-mcp-interface while constants.ts remains inside it will propagate the instability to all 9 consumers simultaneously
- reshape-reason.ts should be pinned to its correct zone (cli-mcp-interface or a dedicated reshape zone) via a sourcevision zone pin, rather than relying on directory co-location in src/analyze/ which makes its zone assignment invisible to filesystem navigation
- [call graph] 1150 internal calls, 305 outgoing, 32 incoming (cohesion: 0.79, coupling: 0.21)

</insights>
